2606.07103.title: Style or Content? Evaluating Style Classifiers with Controlled Content Overlap
2606.07103.paper_summary: Style classifiers can use content cues that correlate with style labels in naturally collected data, yet we lack a systematic way to measure this reliance. We study this problem with a controlled content overlap setup built on parallel Bible translations. Specifically, we define the overlap parameter $α$ as the normalized residual of mutual information between content identity and style label, so that it measures how much content is shared across style classes: from no shared content ($α=0$) to fully shared content ($α=1$). Cross-overlap evaluation of RoBERTa-based classifiers shows that low-overlap models degrade when content cues are removed, while high-overlap models transfer more robustly. A cross-style content retrieval probe further shows that content becomes less recoverable as $α$ increases, with training dynamics showing this removal occurs gradually. Together, these results suggest that controlled overlap provides a simple diagnostic for separating style learning from content shortcuts.
2606.07103.paper_summary_zh: 風格分類器可以使用與自然收集數據中的風格標籤相關的內容線索，但我們缺乏一種系統的方法來衡量這種依賴性。<lb/>我們通過建立在平行聖經翻譯上的受控內容重疊設置來研究這個問題。<lb/>具體而言，我們將重疊參數 $α$ 定義為內容身份和風格標籤之間的互信息的標準化殘差，以便衡量不同風格類別之間共享的內容量：從沒有共享內容 ($α=0$) 到完全共享內容 ($α=1$)。<lb/>基於 RoBERTa 的分類器的交叉重疊評估顯示，當移除內容線索時，低重疊模型的性能下降，而高重疊模型則更具穩健性。<lb/>跨風格內容檢索探針進一步顯示，隨著 $α$ 的增加，內容變得不易恢復，訓練動態顯示這種移除是逐漸發生的。<lb/>綜合這些結果表明，受控重疊提供了一種簡單的診斷方法，用於將風格學習與內容捷徑分開。
2606.07103.author: Zhuo Liu et.al.
2606.07103.authors: Zhuo Liu, Haozheng Du, Xiangxiang Xu, Hangfeng He
